vllm.title: Fix docker python version
vllm.author: NikolaBorisov
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Docker images were broken because we build the extensions with python3.8 but then we try to run them with other version of python.
This results in exception error
  File "/workspace/vllm/model_executor/layers/quantization/awq.py", line 6, in <module>
    from vllm._C import ops
ModuleNotFoundError:

vllm.title: Migrate AquilaForCausalLM to LlamaForCausalLM
vllm.author: esmeetu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
There is no actual difference between AquilaForCausalLM and LlamaForCausalLM.
This is a subsequent PR of #2637.

vllm.title: Don't use cupy NCCL for AMD backends
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt: CuPy doesn't support the recent versions of ROCm (e.g., 5.6 and 5.7) and cupy-rocm-5.0 seems to be incompatible with these recent rocm versions. Hence, we use PyTorch NCCL (RCCL) instead of CuPy NCCL (RCCL) for AMD GPUs.

vllm.title: Align LoRA code between Mistral and Mixtral (fixes #2875)
vllm.author: pcmoritz
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This aligns the LoRA code between Mistral and Mixtral and fixes #2875 as a result.

vllm.title: [BugFix] Fix GC bug for `LLM` class
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR fixes a bug that LLM class is not GC-ed even after gc.collect(), which was introduced by #1804 . It turns out that the bug happens because importing punica kernels raises an exception which is not released until the user uses LoRA.
Reproducible script:
import gc
import torch
from vllm import

vllm.title: Fix decilm.py
vllm.author: pcmoritz
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Since #1804, the DeciLM model was broken because the constructor of LlamaForCausalLM was changed and the change was not reflected in the subclass DeciLMForCausalLM.
This was actually caught by test_models.py, but that test has not been running properly in the CI (it looks like due to low GPU memory)
